OPPOSITION TO DEFT'S M OTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF SPARKS, SHEILA

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT U-HAUL CO. OF CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN AND NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT RAISED. THE ACCIDENT INVOLVING THE TRUCK OCCURRED 6/11/07. (UMF 1). IT IS AGAINST DEFENDANT'S POLICY TO PERMIT VISITORS, CLIENTS, EMPLOYEES, OR ANY OTHER INDIVIDUALS TO USE RENTAL VEHICLES WITHOUT FIRST EXECUTING EITHER A RENTAL AGREEMENT OR AN INTERNAL USE AGREEMENT. (UMF 19) WHENEVER A VEHICLE IS LEASED OR REMOVED FROM THE FACILITY BY AN EMPLOYEE OR CUSTOMER, AN AGREEMENT IS GENERATED. (UMF 19-20) AT THE RELEVANT TIME, THE TRUCK INVOLVED IN THE COLLISION WAS NOT LEASED TO A CUSTOMER OR TAKEN OFF THE PREMISES UNDER AN INTERNAL OR ANY OTHER USE AGREEMENT. (UMF 23-24). THE VEHICLE WAS NOT AUTHORIZED TO BE REMOVED FROM THE PREMISES UNTIL 6/16/07. (UMF 22) THE VEHICLE WAS TAKEN IN A MANNER CONTRARY TO POLICY, WITHOUT PERMISSION. PLAINTIFF PRESENTS NO EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT HER ASSERTION THAT THE VEHICLE WAS TAKEN WITH PERMISSION, BUT RELIES ON SPECULATION.(PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-16-2009 Arbitrator Unable To Serve

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-25-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT U-HAUL CO. OF CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant shifted the burden and no triable issue of material fact raised. The accident involving the truck occurred 6/11/07. (UMF 1). It is against defendant's policy to permit visitors, clients, employees, or any other individuals to use rental vehicles without first executing either a rental agreement or an internal use agreement. (UMF 19) whenever a vehicle is leased or removed from the facility by an employee or customer, an agreement is generated. (UMF 19-20) At the relevant time, the truck involved in the collision was not leased to a customer or taken off the premises under an internal or any other use agreement. (UMF 23-24). The vehicle was not authorized to be removed from the premises until 6/16/07. (UMF 22) The vehicle was taken in a manner contrary to policy, without permission. Plaintiff presents no evidence to support her assertion that the vehicle was taken with permission, but relies on speculation.(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
